name: Gwangju (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: the rising sun of the emblem

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem — a red flying-dove/sun motif with a navy accent over abstract waves — above the city's name in Korean.
history: Gwangju, a Metropolitan City (Gwangyeoksi) since 1995, adopted its current flag in May 1995 along with promotion to special-city status. The emblem is an abstract design symbolising Mudeungsan mountain, the surrounding fields and the bright sun, and recalls Gwangju's identity as a 'city of light' (its name 광주/光州 literally means 'light province/city') and a centre of Korea's pro-democracy movement.

features.symbolism: abstract dove- or sun-and-wave motif in red and navy, evoking the city's name 'light province' (光州) and its role as a centre of Korea's democratic movement
